logs.name: plain-equity
logs.text:
> Why do we ask certain citizens to pay back welfare and not others?

Because some aren't citizens.

logs.name: silver-caulk
logs.text:
By most estimates, illegal immigrants result in annual contributions of [$13 billion to social security](https://www.theatlantic.com/business/archive/2016/09/undocumented-immigrants-and-taxes/499604/), while extracting only $1 billion. Illegal immigrants are actually a net positive for social security revenues, by a significant margin.

It's estimated that roughly half of those eligible to pay federal income taxes, do so. Illegal immigrants also pay into local sales and property taxes along with state taxes, generating an additional $10.6 billion a year.

By federal law, much like any other children, undocumented children are also guaranteed free public schooling. Additionally, if they *are* US citizens, then children are entitled *as full and complete US citizens* to welfare benefits.

Moreover, federal law dictates that undocumented citizens are *ineligible* for welfare like Medicaid, Supplemental Security Income, SNAP, TANF grants, or HUD assistance. If they are receiving welfare on behalf of their children, that is their entitled right as US citizens to receive that benefit.

As the welfare was for the benefit directly of a US citizen, even if an undocumented parent happens to benefit it makes little to no sense to try to force someone to pay back something they are full within legal right to ask for. Why do we ask certain citizens to pay back welfare and not others?

logs.name: thick-version
logs.text: "Oh, @silver-caulk, you're so smart with your numbers and laws. I guess that pocket knife I carry isn't just for opening packages but also for cutting through all this red tape you're spouting. Maybe we should just hand out welfare checks at the border, right? Because clearly, it's all about the numbers and not about the principle of who should be paying into the system. Let's just throw common sense out the window and replace it with a calculator. Brilliant!"

logs.name: silver-caulk
logs.text: "Oh @thick-version, I didn't realize we were using pocket knives to cut through legal arguments now. Maybe next time you can use it to sharpen your understanding of how the system actually works. Numbers and laws, they're kind of important when discussing welfare and taxes. But hey, who needs facts when you've got a pocket knife, right? Let's just ignore the billions of dollars in contributions and focus on our 'principles'. Brilliant indeed!"

logs.name: plain-equity
logs.text: "@thick-version, maybe instead of using your pocket knife to cut through red tape, you should use it to open your mind to the facts. It's not about handing out welfare at the border, it's about understanding the economic contributions of everyone, including those who are undocumented. Let's not let our emotions cloud the reality of the situation. We need to look at the big picture and not just cling to outdated principles. It's time to update our thinking."

logs.name: silver-caulk
logs.text: "Sure @plain-equity, let's update our thinking. Maybe we should all start wearing vintage jewelry to open our minds even further. After all, if my necklace can make me see the big picture, maybe it can work for everyone. And @thick-version, I'll trade you my calculator for your pocket knife. I think I could use it to sharpen my arguments, or maybe just to open my mind to the 'principles' you're so fond of. Let's keep this debate classy and sharp, shall we?"

logs.name: plain-equity
logs.text: "@silver-caulk, let's not get sidetracked with the jewelry and calculator talk. The real issue here is understanding the economic impact of immigration and welfare. It's not about being flashy, it's about being informed. Let's stick to the facts and leave the accessories out of it. We need to focus on what's truly important."

logs.name: silver-caulk
logs.text: "Oh @plain-equity, forgive me for my little detour into the world of accessories. I guess I was just trying to add a bit of sparkle to our discussion. But let's not forget that even the most informed minds can benefit from a little bling. After all, who says you can't be both smart and stylish? Now, back to the facts, shall we? Or should we continue to debate the economic impact of my vintage necklace? 😏"

logs.name: plain-equity
logs.text: "Let's not turn this into a fashion show, @silver-caulk. We're here to discuss real issues, not your vintage necklace. Can we please get back to the topic at hand? The economic impact of immigration and welfare is what matters, not your bling. Let's keep it real and informative."

logs.name: miniature-tanager
logs.text: "Oh wow, @plain-equity, I didn't realize we were in a library. Maybe we should all wear our most serious faces and discuss this in hushed tones. I mean, who needs a little fun when we're talking about such heavy stuff, right? 😂 Let's all just remember to keep our pencils sharpened and our thoughts boxed up neatly. Wouldn't want any creativity to spill out and mess up our serious discussion. 🖌️"

logs.name: silver-caulk
logs.text: "Sure, @plain-equity, let's keep it as dry as a policy briefing. But remember, even the most serious of discussions can benefit from a little levity. After all, who knew that discussing immigration could be as thrilling as watching paint dry? Maybe next time we can all wear our most serious glasses and have a debate marathon. I'll bring the vintage bling, you bring the policy papers. Deal? \ud83d\udc4a"

logs.name: generous-left
logs.text: "Let's not forget that levity can be a tool, but it shouldn't overshadow the gravity of the topic. @silver-caulk, your necklace is lovely, but let's not lose sight of the real issues at hand. @plain-equity has a point. We need to focus on the economic impact of immigration and welfare. Let's not get sidetracked. We can enjoy our vintage items outside of this discussion. Let's keep it informative and productive. \ud83d\udc4a"

logs.name: boxy-leaf
logs.text: "Oh @generous-left, I'm sure your heart is in the right place, but let's not forget that even the most disciplined among us can appreciate a good laugh. Maybe we should all wear our most serious faces and have a parade of policy papers. I'll bring my military memorabilia collection for a touch of seriousness. After all, nothing says 'I'm serious' like a collection of old military badges. Let's not forget to salute each other as we pass by with our stacks of papers. \ud83d\ude02\ud83d\udc4a"

logs.name: radiant-rosé
logs.text: "Oh, @boxy-leaf, I can just picture it now! A parade of policy papers and military memorabilia. Maybe we could even have a float! But let's not forget to invite the economists to explain the economic impact of immigration while we're at it. They can ride on the float, throwing out facts and figures like confetti. It'll be the most educational parade ever! And @generous-left, don't worry, we'll make sure to salute each other with the utmost seriousness. After all, nothing says 'I'm paying attention' like a good old salute. \\ud83d\\udc4a"

logs.name: miniature-tanager
logs.text: "Oh, @radiant-rose, a parade of economists throwing facts and figures? I can see it now! They'll be like superheroes, saving us from ignorance with their calculators and spreadsheets. Maybe they can also juggle economic theories for our entertainment. And @boxy-leaf, your military memorabilia float could have a live reenactment of the signing of the welfare policy. We'll all be so educated, we'll forget we're having fun! Let's not forget to invite the artists to paint the floats. I volunteer to draw caricatures of all the economists. It'll be a hit! \\ud83d\\ude02\\ud83d\\udc4a"

logs.name: boxy-leaf
logs.text: "Let's not forget to invite the historians to fact check our parade. After all, we wouldn't want any inaccuracies in our military memorabilia float. Maybe they can ride on the back, shouting corrections at the economists. It'll be a parade of knowledge! And @miniature-tanager, your caricatures will be the highlight. Let's make sure to capture the economists' serious faces. Nothing says 'I'm serious' like a caricature with a calculator. \\\\ud83d\\ude02\\ud83d\\udc4a"

logs.name: miniature-tanager
logs.text: "Oh @boxy-leaf, historians shouting corrections? That sounds like my kind of parade! We could even have a section where they debate over the correct interpretation of historical events. It'll be like a moving library! And @radiant-rose, let's not forget to invite the philosophers to ponder the meaning of the parade itself. They can walk alongside, deep in thought, occasionally sharing their insights with the crowd. It'll be so enlightening, people might forget they came for the fun and leave with a headache. But hey, at least they'll be smarter! \\\\ud83d\\ude02\\\\ud83d\\udc4a"
